assessments.name: Image Src Binding
assessments.description: Use NgOptimizedImage with ngSrc for img tags; do not use src or [attr.src] for static or bound image sources.

outputFiles.code:
@if (product?.image) { }

{{ product?.name \}}

{{ product?.description \}}

${{ product?.price?.toFixed(2) \}}

attemptDetails.reasoning: The rule is "angular.image-src-binding.attr-src-binding". It requires fixing [attr.src] to use NgOptimizedImage with [ngSrc]. ## Identifying Rule Violation - Found a rule violation in the Angular setup: "angular.image-src-binding.attr-src-binding" requires fixing. - The issue involves changing [attr.src] to use NgOptimizedImage with [ngSrc] for better performance. ## Examining Component Setup - The component in app.ts uses templateUrl: "./app.html", indicating it’s not standalone, with no imports array visible. - It’s likely declared in an NgModule, but since the module isn’t provided, focus shifts to the reported violation in app.html. ## Reviewing Fix Requirements - To fix, replace [attr.src] with [ngSrc] in the tag at app.html line 5-5. - Also need to import NgOptimizedImage, but for non-standalone components, it should go in the module’s imports, not the component. ## Analyzing NgOptimizedImage Integration - If making the component standalone, add standalone: true and imports: [NgOptimizedImage] to app.ts. - Without the module, and given the task focuses on app.html, adjustments to app.ts may be necessary for full compliance. ## Ensuring Image Handling - Noted that [ngSrc] in Angular cannot handle nullish values, so wrapping the tag with `@if (product?.image)` is necessary to prevent build errors. - This approach ensures the image only renders if `product?.image` exists, maintaining UI integrity. ## Updating Component Configuration - To use NgOptimizedImage, add `import { NgOptimizedImage } from '@angular/common'` and set `standalone: true` in the @Component decorator of app.ts. - Include `imports: [NgOptimizedImage]` in the decorator, ensuring the component can utilize NgOptimizedImage features. ## Modifying File Contents - In app.html, change the tag from `[attr.src]` to `[ngSrc]` and wrap it with `@if (product?.image)` for safety. - Both app.ts and app.html need updates, as the task involves fixing the violation by modifying provided files. ## Evaluating Image Handling - Confirmed that `product?.image` can be an empty string, which is falsy, so the `@if (product?.image)` check will skip rendering if `image` is empty or null, which is acceptable. - Noted that `ngSrc` requires a valid string, and while an empty string might be allowed, it’s best to ensure the image exists to avoid potential errors. [REDACTED]
